headline: Navy Surface Fleet Readiness Falls Below 60 Percent
context: TLDM reported only 58% operational readiness across surface fleet with 18 of 44 vessels in maintenance or awaiting repairs.

cards.big: Navy readiness at only 58% with 18 of 44 vessels in maintenance
cards.sub: Maritime defence capability critically degraded by maintenance backlogs

cards.big: 6 Kedah-class patrol vessels average 18 years old with 3 non-operational
cards.sub: Replacement programme delayed 4 years by budget and procurement disputes

cards.big: LCS programme delivered 0 of 6 vessels after RM6.1 billion spent
cards.sub: Boustead Naval Shipyard project is costliest defence procurement failure

cards.big: Average repair time increased from 6 months to 14 months for major systems
cards.sub: Foreign-sourced spare parts face supply chain delays and currency costs

cards.big: Fleet readiness tests whether maintenance investment matches operational requirements
Below 60% surface fleet readiness means the navy cannot simultaneously patrol all territorial waters.
